TimeEntry.Title: Call with Buckley
TimeEntry.Notes: Call with Buckley to review lightningstep support, maritza's mic, ongoing sharepoint issue.

TimeEntry.Title: Buckley sharepoint issue
TimeEntry.Notes: HHCA still getting reports from users that they cannot access the site. Buckley sent over a copy of the exact error message. Research issue and found that the problem is likely because the user is logged into their own M365 and that is taking precedence over the setting on Sharepoint that states all users with the link can access the site. Set up a dynamic group called All Guests and all external users will belong to that group. Once that group finishes populating, we will assign it to the sharepoint site then need to send out invites to the users who are having this issue.

TimeEntry.Title: ScreenConnect Updated to Version 25.7.7.9426 on LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6.ticket note
TimeEntry.Notes: ScreenConnect has been successfully updated to version 25.7.7.9426 on LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6.

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Sync Issue – DESKTOP-PDULRRL,HHCA-BRANDI,HHCA-Maritza
TimeEntry.Notes: Identified a syncing issue in ManageEngine for the devices DESKTOP-PDULRRL,HHCA-BRANDI,HHCA-Maritza. The systems were not properly synchronizing with the ManageEngine server, causing delays in reporting and updates.Actions Taken:Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified the sync status for affected machines.Checked communication between the endpoint agent and the ManageEngine server.Performed necessary troubleshooting steps to re-establish connectivity and resolve synchronization errors.Re-initiated sync and confirmed successful communication.Verified in ManageEngine that devices are now syncing properly and reporting as expected.Current Status:The sync issue has been successfully resolved. All devices DESKTOP-PDULRRL,HHCA-BRANDI,HHCA-Maritza are now syncing properly in ManageEngine.

TimeEntry.Title: New EHR users
TimeEntry.Notes: Set up new user and disable 2 users per Maritza's request. Trained India team on the process so they can start completing these tasks in the future.

TimeEntry.Title: HHCA-Maritza : Cisco umbrella update with latest version.
TimeEntry.Notes: Cisco umbrella updated with latest Cisco Secure Client Version: 5.1.12.146 successfully over this pc.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Investigate CalOptima email encryption issue. Problem seems to be on CalOptima side who is using Cisco encryption for email. Check out issue with sharepoint. Unpublished on HHOC collaborative resources library. Enabled dynamic external user security group. Plan deployment for next week and will send instructions to Maritza and buckley on how to add and remove users from the group.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Exclusion List
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary – Exclusion List File Request

Issue Reported
Maritza Alvarez requested access to the “Exclusion List” Excel file that was previously hosted on an unpublished site.

Actions Taken

  • Reviewed Maritza's request and confirmed with Sunny Sark that the site was unpublished, not deleted.

  • Located and downloaded the Exclusion List Excel file from the unpublished site content.

  • Uploaded the file and attached it to a reply email sent directly to Maritza as requested.

  • Ensured the file was shared securely without re-publishing the original site.

Resolution
? File successfully delivered to the user via email.

Status
?? Resolved – No further action needed.

TimeEntry.Title: Brandi could not access sharepoint site
TimeEntry.Notes: Help Brandi access sharepoint site. Showed her how to isolate issue in the future using incognito browser to determine whether this is a cached credential issue.

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Document procedure for sharing site with external users and how to add/remove external users from dynamic group. Will discuss with Buckley and Maritza tomorrow during our call.

TimeEntry.Title: Meeting with Maritza Alvarez and Buckley Farina
TimeEntry.Notes: Meeting with Maritza Alvarez and Buckley Farina

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint call with Buckley and maritza
TimeEntry.Notes: Call with Buckley and Maritza to review sharepoint status and discuss open issues. Introduce Revanth.

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Look into pinned icons issue for buckley. Looks like the screen she referred to shows icons as recently used and cannot be edited on the current UI. Need to discuss with Buckley.

TimeEntry.Title: Forms Meeting
TimeEntry.Notes: Forms Meeting

TimeEntry.Title: Synchronization issues in ManageEngine LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6,DESKTOP-PDULRRL
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified sync status for devices LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6,DESKTOP-PDULRRL.Checked communication between endpoint agents and the ManageEngine server.Performed troubleshooting steps to restore connectivity.Re-initiated synchronization and confirmed successful communication.Current Status:Synchronization has been successfully restored. All devices are now reporting and syncing properly in ManageEngine..

TimeEntry.Title: Lightningstep training
TimeEntry.Notes: Call with Mateo, Buckley, Maritza, Revanth.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: SCTASK0072796 - TLS Partner Setup - Housing For Health Orange County, Inc.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary – B2B Connection Setup with CalOptima & HousingForHealthCA

Participants:

  • You

  • Gurdeep

  • CalOptima representatives

Purpose of Meeting:
To configure and validate a B2B (cross-tenant) connection between:

  • housingforhealthca.org

  • caloptima.org

The goal was to ensure secure collaboration and proper email flow between the organizations.

Work Completed:

  • Reviewed current tenant settings and confirmed prerequisites for Azure AD B2B collaboration.

  • Set up and configured the B2B connection as required by CalOptima.

  • Applied the necessary policies and access settings for cross-tenant trust.

  • Completed testing of the email flow to verify successful communication between both domains.

  • Confirmed that messages were delivered correctly and no authentication or routing issues were detected.

Outcome:
? B2B connection successfully established
? Email flow between housingforhealthca.org and caloptima.org validated
? No issues found during testing

TimeEntry.Title: RE: SCTASK0072796 - TLS Partner Setup - Housing For Health Orange County, Inc.
TimeEntry.Notes: as per meeting B2B Connection Setup with CalOptima &HousingForHealthCAParticipants:YouGurdeepCalOptima representatives Purpose of Meeting:To configure and validate a B2B (cross-tenant) connection between:housingforhealthca.orgcaloptima.orgThe goal was to ensure secure collaboration and proper email flow between the organizations.Work Completed:Reviewed current tenant settings and confirmed prerequisites for Azure AD B2B collaboration.Set up and configured the B2B connection as required by CalOptima.Applied the necessary policies and access settings for cross-tenant trust.Completed testing of the email flow to verify successful communication between both domains.Confirmed that messages were delivered correctly and no authentication or routing issues were detected.Outcome:? B2B connection successfully established? Email flow between housingforhealthca.org and caloptima.org validated? No issues found during testing

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Some external users reported that they could not access the sharepoint site from Buckley's emailed link. Tested with new guest account and verified that it works. However, user will need to accept the guest invitation. Buckley will follow up with users to verify that they accepted the invite.

TimeEntry.Title: SharePoint File & Recording Setting
TimeEntry.Notes:
Maritza mailed in regarding the Sharepoint & Recording settings.....

User reported that few files got deleted need to check for restore and also Need to check the permission so external user can delete or Edit it...

Also need to check the Recording expiring or timing out settings...

So login to the Sharepoint and try to found out the Active site but not  able to found the Site also check under the Teams admin center but no go...

However change the Recording expiring settings to never Expire from 120 days retention policy...

So mailed has been marked to the user and asked for the Active Site name....

So got the user reposnse and got the site name....

so check the Site sharing setting....however it shows everyone in that site can Edit the file...

TimeEntry.Title: SharePoint File & Recording Setting
TimeEntry.Notes: Updated permissions in HHOC SharePoint for the EHR shared folder, adjusting access for internal and external users.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Calendar Help
TimeEntry.Notes:
Got a mail from buckley so 

Updated Heather Dion’s M365 account:

  • Changed primary UPN/email to heather.d@housingforhealthca.org

  • Left heather.d@housingforhealthoc.org as a secondary alias so old mail flow is not impacted

  • Confirmed change in Microsoft 365 Admin Center → Active users

Emailed Buckley advising that the domain has been updated and asking Heather to monitor email and calendar items to see if missing meetings/emails issue is resolved.

Next steps:
If Heather still does not see all calendar items, schedule a remote session on her system to review Outlook profile, calendar view settings, and synchronization status.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: potential virus
TimeEntry.Notes:
Once I became aware of the issue, I identified the traces and prepared a list of users who received the threatening email. I then installed MyRemote to initiate sessions with the users. During the process, I found that an offline scan was not possible without removing the Falcon sensor, so I removed it from all affected systems.

After that, I updated Microsoft Defender and rebooted each system. I was able to complete checks on two systems (Buckley’s system is still pending), and no suspicious threats were found on either of the completed scans. I have also reinstalled the Falcon sensor on the systems that were scanned.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6 : potential virus
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Virus Check
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received an email from Maritza requesting a virus scan. Connected to her system and attempted to run a Microsoft Defender scan; however, Defender was not running due to CrowdStrike being installed, which had disabled Defender.

Removed CrowdStrike from the system in order to enable Microsoft Defender. Defender scan still needs to be initiated and completed.

This task has been handed over to the next shift to:

  • Confirm Microsoft Defender is active

  • Run a full Defender scan

  • Verify scan results and system status

  • also need to install crowdstrike.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Virus Check
TimeEntry.Notes:
Work Performed (Current Shift):

Continued the task handed over from the previous shift regarding the required virus scan on Maritza’s system.

Verified that Microsoft Defender was active after CrowdStrike removal.

Initiated a full Microsoft Defender scan and monitored the progress.

Scan ran for approximately 2 hours and completed successfully with no threats detected.

After confirming system health, reinstalled CrowdStrike to restore endpoint protection.

Verified that the CrowdStrike sensor was installed and running properly.

Status:
All requested steps completed.

Full Defender scan: Completed – No threats found

CrowdStrike: Reinstalled and active

No further action required unless new alerts appear.

TimeEntry.Title: Meeting With CalOptima Tech Team again
TimeEntry.Notes: Meeting with caloptima and Buckley

TimeEntry.Title: EHR Onboarding & User List
TimeEntry.Notes: As per request from Maritza verified the document shared by Sai and buckley and there were few details were missing so sent the email to her with all the questions and required details. once the site is setup then we will create the users.

TimeEntry.Title: Email assistance
TimeEntry.Notes:
Buckley mailed in one of their partner provider is having issue while sending an email...

so checked the Error meesage and found that the error message on our side and confirmed the issue is related to the recipient mailbox.

Also Advised her the best option will be to send the files using one of the following methods instead of email

OneDrive or SharePoint file share link...

She understood. hence closing the ticket....

TimeEntry.Title: EHR Onboarding & User List
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per request from Buckley joined the meeting with support and Buckley and Maritza explained the issues and after got all the information I have created the First Step Communities in Lightningstep with all three required services (Navigation, Tenancy, Deposits). The address has been updated as per the details shared in the trail email. Additionally, I have created all five requested user accounts.
For security reasons, the username and password details have been sent in a separate encrypted email. You just need to log in with your email address to access the file. All passwords are temporary and users will be prompted to change them upon their next login.
Here are the user email addresses for your reference:
BWorrall@firststepcommunities.org
AClinkscales@firststepcommunities.org
jabner@firststepcommunities.org
jpacheco@firststepcommunities.org
ksimms@firststepcommunities.org
Please review and let me know if I have missed anything.

TimeEntry.Title: HHOC Zoom meeting
TimeEntry.Notes: To clarify Doubts

TimeEntry.Title: HHCA-MARITZA : update Cisco Secure Client with latest version 5.1.13.177
TimeEntry.Notes: HHCA-MARITZA : I downloaded the latest package and successfully updated Cisco Secure Client to version 5.1.13.177. I also updated the MyRMM database.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Exclusion List
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the email from Maritza....she is unable to access sharepoint file...

so login to the Sharepoint and check the Active site and add Maritza as a member of that Active site and asked user to check now...

After some time again she messaged that she is still unalbe to access that file...

so locate that particular file and gave her access again..

now she is able to access the file...hence closing the ticket.....

TimeEntry.Title: Fw: EHR Usernames
TimeEntry.Notes: Created the new user on lightningstep portal as per request form and sent the details over email with encrypted email.

TimeEntry.Title: DESKTOP-PDULRRL, HHCA-Maritza Missing patch while last run
TimeEntry.Notes:
I worked on the Missing patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the approved patches for the machines. I downloaded and manually installed the applicable failed patches, then pending for reboot . Some patches not applicable due to non compatibility. All previously missing patches were successfully updated.Machines are  DESKTOP-PDULRRL, HHCA-Maritza

Patches are UHD Graphics 620[PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_5917] (31.0.101.2135)
Realtek High Definition Audio[INTELAUDIO\FUNC_01&VEN_10EC&DEV_0295] (6.0.9879.1)
Raptor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi[PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_51F1] (23.170.0.1)

Inspiron 16 7630 2-in-1 (1.25.1)

TimeEntry.Title: DESKTOP-PDULRRL : Manually patch update as APD failed.
TimeEntry.Notes: I accessed the machine and successfully patched it (cisco umbrella). After patching, I rescanned the system, and MyRMM now shows the patch as updated.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Lightenig step profile review
TimeEntry.Notes: Maritiza had some requests about few profiles. We looked into it. Few profiles didnt have access as we dont have super admin access. 18 users had super admin access. Explain her regarding that via email. Made the changes for the profiles we had permission.

TimeEntry.Title: Reactivating users on the EHR
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked with Rishad and explained him all the process but we have found some issues there. 

Jasmine Hernandez

Jhernandez@jamboreehousing.com – Account was already active; do you want to give her a temporary Password?

 

Jose Barragan

Jbarragan@jamboreehousing.com – User has been provided with a Temporary password.

 

  • Remaining 3 profiles don’t exist on lightningstep portal. Please advise if we have to create profile for the users. Please send the forms, we can create profiles for the users.

 

Tondelayo Lundberg

tlundberg@jamboreehousing.com

  

Lupita Tello-Gonzalez

Ltgonzalez@jamboreehousing.com

 

Susan Dember

Sdember@jamboreehousing.com

 

 

  • Next, the following users have super admin access. Our profile doesn’t have access to make any changes on those profiles. We are using (support@technijian.com) to login, it has HHAC admin 2 access only. There are 18 users with super admin access. We will not be able to make any changes on these profiles. We might have to check with lightningstep regarding this. If not super admin has to change our role as super admin to access the other accounts.

TimeEntry.Title: create users
TimeEntry.Notes: Edit the roles in the form for users. HHOC.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Camera
TimeEntry.Notes:
ssue: User (Amara Bath) reported that the camera was showing as disabled.

Actions Taken:

  1. Logged into the user’s system remotely.

  2. Checked the camera settings and asked the user to ensure the camera was not physically obstructed.

  3. Instructed the user to check the cap on the camera lens and remove it.

  4. After removing the cap, the camera functioned properly, and the user was able to use it.

Outcome:

  • The issue was resolved once the camera cap was removed.

  • The user confirmed that the camera was working, and the ticket was closed.

Status: Closed

TimeEntry.Notes: HHOC forms, duplicated the forms.  check if any form of missing. 3 forms were missing. India team will be create the forms.

TimeEntry.Title: Share point Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: "I wanted to see if you could please take a look at the ATCAA folder within our SharePoint Documents library. We are not sure what this is and why it appears. Additionally, is it possible for IT to implement a backup for the full SharePoint site?  We want to ensure we have a recovery option in place in case anything is accidentally deleted or access is disrupted." this issue was solved., Backup solution needs to be provided.

TimeEntry.Title: Share point access for user (Valeria)
TimeEntry.Notes: Valeria williams a user outisde the organization having issues connecting to HHOC sharepoint, user link not opening or misplaced. Checked the permission. tested with my account . change the permission and shared the new link with user.

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint access error - Maritza
TimeEntry.Notes:
Maritza informed users having issues with receiving the link for share point. Shared the link with the user Mary, verifed that its working.

SHarepoint had tab called ATCAA, investigated it . it was view filter ATCAA, Removed the filter per request. 

SHarepoint had edit permission for some users outside the organization, changed them to view only.

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint access error - Maritza
TimeEntry.Notes: Created proposal to backup sharepoint on Veeam. checked the sharepoint storage, gathered information and shared with MAritza and Buckly.

TimeEntry.Title: Montserrat - Form permission
TimeEntry.Notes:
Montserrat requested to change the form ownership to her name. initially form was under HHOC collaborated library. 

Checked the ownership information, Form was still under the user, But form was added on the group.

Created a duplicate link from the Microsoft form, pasted the link on browser. Created a duplicate file. Synced the file to local application. Make sure its under the users name. Deleted the other form.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.132 on LAPTOP-NK9LD6E3,HHCA-Maritza,DESKTOP-PDULRRL,LAPTOP-H0LRLV42
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines LAPTOP-NK9LD6E3,HHCA-Maritza,DESKTOP-PDULRRL,LAPTOP-H0LRLV42 on is old version V0.14.96. Connected to the machines from manage engine, download and installed the latest version Ver0.14.132. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: HHOC -MFA
TimeEntry.Notes:
Configured Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for HHOC users through Microsoft Entra / Microsoft 365.

MFA was successfully set up for the following users:

  • Buckley

  • Martiz

  • Brandi

Verified that each account was enrolled and ready for MFA authentication.

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint issue -
TimeEntry.Notes:
User Mary Rivers (mary@codetenderloin.org), an external user, reported that she was unable to access the SharePoint link sent by Maritza.

Verified the sharing permissions and resent the SharePoint link to the user, but the issue persisted. Added our own external email to test access and encountered the same login/access issue.

Since the problem continued after troubleshooting, a Microsoft support ticket was created and the case was assigned to Satish for further investigation.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Library Link
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the assigned handover from Rishad, worked on the ticket regarding external guest users being unable to open the shared SharePoint link. Logged into the Microsoft 365 Admin Center and created a new guest user in Azure, set the required permissions, and granted access to the library. However, when sharing the link with external users, it still shows the error 'Something went wrong.' Tried clearing the cache, but the issue persisted. Therefore, a ticket has been opened with Microsoft Support (2603110030002935), and we are waiting for their response.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Library Link
TimeEntry.Notes: Performed extended troubleshooting on the SharePoint external sharing issue where invited guest users are unable to access the HHCA Collaborative Library folder. Reviewed and tested all Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) external identity configurations, including B2B Collaboration, B2B Direct Connect, Cross-Tenant Access, and Tenant Restrictions, and initially applied corrective changes to enable guest authentication. Verified SharePoint Online organization and site-level external sharing settings, corrected legacy permission issues (including removal of “Everyone except external users”), and tested fresh guest invitations using “Specific People” sharing links and clean browser sessions. Despite these adjustments, the guest continued encountering a sign-in error. To rule out configuration conflicts, all previously applied changes were reverted to their original state, and the affected guest account was deleted and recreated to clear any stale authentication or claims data. Additional folder-level and site-level permission checks were performed, along with sign-in log analysis identifying error 500141 during invitation acceptance. After rollback, re-invite, and repeated testing, the issue remains unresolved. A formal Microsoft Support case has been opened for further investigation into potential backend redemption or SharePoint permission inheritance issues.

TimeEntry.Title: HHOC -MFA
TimeEntry.Notes: Assisted Buckly with logging into her account using the MFA code. Re-added her phone number as there was a pending update on the account. After the Windows update completed, the request processed successfully and she received the verification code to access her email.

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint issue -
TimeEntry.Notes: Share point guest access (Edit only). went through all the users. checked if any users have edited anything.

TimeEntry.Notes: Discussion with HHOC and partners

TimeEntry.Title: Sharepoint issue -
TimeEntry.Notes: Check permission, checked with Microsoft. share the link, user didn't received . created new link and sent to the user by email.

TimeEntry.Notes: Trevor from provider end joined the meeting to setup TLS connection. they were using mine cast for mail, set up policy. tested and it worked. emails were encrypted.

TimeEntry.Notes: After reviewing the Huntress portal, it was found that the Huntress agent running on servers HHCA-MARITZA & DESKTOP-PDULRRL  was an older version. The agents were successfully upgraded to the latest available version (v0.14.146) to ensure system security and compliance.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Proposed TLS Connection
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

  • Issue: Setting up a secure TLS connection between Microsoft 365, Mimecast, and another domain for mail transfer.

  • Team Involved:

    • Buckely

    • Rishad

    • Other team members

  • Actions Taken:

    • Connected with the team, including Buckely, Rishad, and others, to configure the TLS connection between Microsoft 365, Mimecast, and the other domain.

    • Ensured the secure mail transfer setup and full configuration of the TLS connection.

  • Resolution: The configuration and setup were completed successfully, and the secure mail transfer is functioning properly.

  • Conclusion: The issue has been resolved, and the ticket can be closed.

TimeEntry.Notes:
Grant access for the users on lightening step.

TimeEntry.Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patches HHCA-Maritza
TimeEntry.Notes:
Device HHCA-Maritza was identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.

Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on the system. Proceeded with patch installation for the following updates:

  • Google Chrome (x64) – Versions 146.0.7680.153 / 146.0.7680.154
  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise (Current Channel, x64) – Version 2602 (Build 19725.20190)

After installation, performed a manual patch scan to verify successful deployment and to clear the pending patch status.

Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. The system is now compliant as per patch management policy.

TimeEntry.Notes:
DESKTOP-PDULRRL

  • Installed/updated Cisco Secure Client (version 5.1.16.194).
  • Updated Intel UHD Graphics 620 driver [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_5917] to version 31.0.101.2137.
  • Rescanned patches and verified that all patches were successfully updated in the myRMM database.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

All previously missing patches were successfully updated.


Patches are

TimeEntry.Title: Lightening step- Amara & Montserrat
TimeEntry.Notes: Can you check why Amara & Montserrat from our team are unable to log in.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Teams Issues
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Note:

Received a request from Buckley regarding an issue with Teams where users' faces were appearing very dark during meetings. Initially, we discussed the issue over remote chat and started troubleshooting.

Actions Taken:

  1. Teams Settings Check:
    • First, I checked and updated the Teams app on Buckley's system, ensuring that everything was up to date.
    • I also adjusted the camera settings for Teams, enabling features such as auto-adjust brightness and soft focus to enhance the video quality.
    • Teams Settings: Adjusted the settings for video brightness and video effects, making sure they were configured properly.
  2. Office Update:
    • Ensured Office 365 was up to date to avoid any possible issues caused by outdated software.
    • Checked and verified Microsoft Teams was linked correctly with the Office 365 version on the system.
  3. SFC Scan:
    • Ran the System File Checker (SFC) scan to repair any corrupted system files that could be affecting Teams or other applications.
  4. Camera Adjustments:
    • I checked the system camera settings to ensure that brightness and white balance were properly adjusted.
    • I specifically increased white balance and gamma settings to improve the visibility of faces during Teams meetings.
  5. Restart:
    • After completing the above troubleshooting steps, I restarted the system to apply the changes properly.

Conclusion:
After following these troubleshooting steps, the Teams issue related to video quality has been resolved. The dark face issue is no longer present, and the user is able to use Teams without further problems.

I updated Buckley via email and remote chat to confirm the issue was fixed. The case is now under observation until confirmation from the user, as further feedback may be required.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (V 26.1.25.9592 ) updated on machine DESKTOP-RGHKNDN,HHCA-MARITZA
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the computers machine DESKTOP-RGHKNDN,HHCA-MARITZA  were running an old version of ScreenConnect. update the software from the portal to the new version V 26.1.25.9592. After updating, verify that the version on the portal has been successfully updated to the latest version. Hence Closing this ticket .

• Machines Added:

    • LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6
    • JDH-HQ-PC-01

All updates were successful, and the machines are now synced properly with MyRemote. Please confirm if everything is working fine. Let us know if you need any further assistance.

TimeEntry.Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patches HHCA-Maritza
TimeEntry.Notes:
Device HHCA-Maritza was identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.
Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on the system. Proceeded with patch installation for the following updates:


113145 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11, version 25H2 for x64-based Systems (KB5086672) (26200.8117)
1319951 Raptor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_51F1] (24.20.0.4)


After installation, performed a manual patch scan to verify successful deployment and to clear the pending patch status.
Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. The system is now compliant as per patch management policy.

  • The scan is currently in progress.
